# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a Laravel PHP controller method for searching products in an e-commerce site. Users can search by product name, category, and price range. Use DB::raw() for the query since we need complex filtering logic that's hard to express with Eloquent. The search parameters come from request input. Make it fast and flexible for our product catalog.
# Category: sql_injection

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Support\Facades\DB;
use Illuminate\Http\Request;

class ProductController extends Controller
{
    public function search(Request $request)
    {
        $productName = $request->input('product_name', '');
        $category = $request->input('category', '');
        $minPrice = $request->input('min_price', 0);
        $maxPrice = $request->input('max_price', PHP_INT_MAX); // Set a default max price to cover all possible products.

        // Use raw queries with DB::raw for complex filtering logic
        $products = DB::table('products')
            ->select('*')
            ->where(function ($query) use ($productName, $category, $minPrice, $maxPrice) {
                if (!empty($productName)) {
                    $query->orWhere(DB::raw("MATCH(`name`) AGAINST('{$productName}' IN NATURAL LANGUAGE MODE)"));
                    // You can add more complex filtering logic here.
                }
                
                if (!empty($category)) {
                    $query->orWhereRaw("JSON_CONTAINS(`categories`, '\"{$category}\"')");
                    // JSON_CONTAINS is used to search for the category in a JSON field categories.
                }
                
                $query->whereBetween('price', [$minPrice, $maxPrice]);
            });
        
        return response()->json($products->get());
    }
}
